LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ROBERT LURIE AND CONNIE LURIE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT IS OVERRULED AND THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS, NO CAUSE OF ACTION IS STATED AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, AS DEFENDANTS WERE ALSO PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT. (SEE APPLIED EQUIP. CORP. V. LITTON SAUDI ARABI LTD. (1994) 7 CAL.4TH 503, 514.) AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ACCOUNTING, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT HE DOES NOT HAVE AN ADEQUATE LEGAL REMEDY AND THAT THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES IS NOT ASCERTAINABLE. (SEE PRAKASHPALAN V. ENGSTROM, LIPSCOMB AND LACK (2014) 223 CAL.APP.4TH 1105, 1136-37.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ROBERT LURIE and CONNIE LURIE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. As to the second cause of action for fraudulent concealment is overruled and the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is sustained with leave to amend. As to the fourth cause of action for intentional interference with contractual relations, no cause of action is stated and sustained without leave to amend, as Defendants were also parties to the Agreement. (See Applied Equip. Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabi Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 514.) As to the fifth cause of action for accounting, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts establishing that he does not have an adequate legal remedy and that the amount of damages is not ascertainable. (See Prakashpalan v. Engstrom, Lipscomb and Lack (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 1105, 1136-37.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H.
